description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular wooden cabinet and four angled, tapered legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. The front features a large, black screen bordered by a cream-colored frame, with a control panel below that includes several knobs, switches, and a prominent rotary dial. The sides and back are finished in a dark wood veneer, with ventilation slots and a recessed handle for carrying or repositioning. The rear panel also displays additional venting and what appear to be input/output ports, typical of older television designs. The overall construction suggests a combination of wood, metal, and plastic, with a glass screen at the front. This television would likely be found in retro-themed urban interiors, vintage shops, or as a decorative piece in public spaces.
description: A vintage, mid-century television with a wooden cabinet, cream front panel, and four angled legs, featuring a large black screen and classic control knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large black screen, cream control panel with knobs and switches, and the four angled legs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the dark wood veneer, rectangular shape, and a recessed handle near the top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the dark wood finish, ventilation slots, and a panel with possible input/output ports.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the wood grain and another recessed handle.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick cream-colored bezel, rectangular, plastic material, with a simple and functional style.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of knobs and switches on the lower front panel, arranged in a row.
